behavior_surfaces: workflow runtime phase advancement and prompt assembly
WebSocket workflow transition and auto-continue prompt
state_contracts: Workflow phase transitions accept optional nextPhaseContextStrategy with values inherit or clear.
Default and omitted strategy remain inherited context for backward compatibility.
The clear strategy is opt-in from confirmation-time UI and only applies when advancing to a next phase.
The clear strategy does not delete transcript history, workflow companion state, handoff artifacts, output artifacts, final reports, or audit records.

When clear is selected, the next active phase prompt includes a context boundary instructing the agent to use accepted handoff materials and prior workflow artifacts rather than inherited transcript history.
Manual completion and pending confirmation both support the same clear next-phase strategy.
Final phase confirmation does not expose a next-phase context checkbox because no next phase exists.

verification.command: git diff --check -- <touched workflow context reset paths>
verification.result: passed with Windows LF/CRLF conversion warnings only

known_unknowns: The working tree contained many workflow-related dirty changes before this quick task; this task worked with them and did not revert unrelated edits.
Full bun run verify was not run; focused tests plus check:server and check:desktop passed.
A running desktop sidecar may need rebuild/restart before bundled runtime and UI reflect the source changes.
Project cognition entry was review/partial_refresh and selected broad workflow coverage-gap concept N-030, so live code and tests are the source of truth for completion claims.
confidence_notes: Runtime tests prove clear strategy persists a next-phase marker while preserving accepted handoff artifact refs.
REST and WebSocket tests prove transition request propagation and auto-continue prompt behavior.
Desktop component tests prove the checkbox sends clear only when checked, is absent for final phase, and works for manual completion.
No changed runtime/API/control file introduced destructive transcript or workflow artifact deletion.
user_decisions: User chose confirmation-time placement for the clear-context option.
User accepted an unchecked default/inherit behavior and a checkbox that means the next phase starts from handoff materials rather than full prior conversation.

boundary.scope: Add opt-in clear next-phase context strategy at workflow transition confirmation time.
excluded: Deleting transcript history or workflow companion artifacts
